steps.title: Identify the transition between different moons
steps.content: The passage follows a hypothetical spaceship journey visiting Jupiter's moons: first Elara (with a prograde orbit), then Pasiphae (another Jovian moon). The transition word must signal the shift from one moon to a different moon.

steps.title: Apply the location-change signal
steps.content: ”Elsewhere” signals a different location, fitting the transition from Elara to Pasiphae. The other three choices suggest elaboration on the prograde concept, not a location shift. That matches choice D — D is correct.
